verbatim_text: But actually when we look at how well developers were using this very same model in tools like Cursor, these are some of the examples I saw on Reddit with hundreds of upvotes of people being disappointed or struggling to actually use this very model in their real world workflows. And so my research is interested in how we build language model agents for these collaborative workflows. In particular my approach is I argue that instead of focusing all of our efforts on just how we're developing better models of course we want to be able to build you know better like post-training and evaluation on top of strong base models but actually shifting and thinking about the second half of the pipeline which is once we actually deploy these models in practice what are the new challenges that come up in terms of things like interactive evaluation getting feedback from users the kinds of interfaces that shape interactions and so on. And of course then also some of the talk will talk about like you know how the two can be more synergistic as well.
